displayTitle: Half-Life: The Clock You Can't Argue With

beats.narration: A gamma source is billions of unstable atoms decaying on a schedule so dependable you can plan every exposure around it.

payload.title: RADIOACTIVE DECAY
payload.sub: How source strength runs on the calendar.

beats.narration: Activity is decay rate. One becquerel is one disintegration per second; field sources run gigabecquerels to terabecquerels; one curie is thirty-seven gigabecquerels.

payload.heading: Activity: how fast the source decays

beats.narration: Iridium one ninety-two halves about every seventy-four days: one hundred curies becomes fifty, then twenty-five. On semilog paper, decay plots as a dead straight line.

payload.heading: Half-life: the fixed clock
payload.note: ON SEMILOG PAPER, DECAY IS A STRAIGHT LINE

beats.narration: Before the first shot: read the decay chart for today's curies, then stretch exposure time in proportion — half the curies, twice the minutes.

payload.heading: Before the first shot of the shift

chips.t: Half the curies, twice the minutes

beats.narration: The rule never changes: after one half-life, half remains. After two, a quarter. After three, an eighth. The calendar always wins.
